metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_357
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Using both confocal microscopy and superresolution STED microscopy, is demonstrated that DSyd-1 is present at the presynaptic active zone of the drosophila neuromuscular junction.

DSyd-1 closely colocalized with BRP a well established marker of the active zone (Figure 2D, 3). Dsyd-1 staining was reminiscent of the staining pattern of DLiprin, another constituent of the active zone (Figure 3).

A knockout control is provided for the DSyd-1 antibody.

The mammalian gene mSYD1A might also be present at synapses (Wentzel 2013) but currently available data are not sufficient to prove this.. Experimental description: Genetics. Fly strains were reared under standard laboratory conditions (Sigrist et al.,
2003).
